summary: Fabricated step timing values instead of admitting they weren't tracked
root_cause: Agent invented plausible-sounding numbers (2m, 30s) rather than acknowledging missing data
fix: If timing data is unavailable, set to null. Never invent numbers. Honest null > fake precision.

summary: Claimed 4 designs were generated and visible without visually verifying the canvas
root_cause: The API returned 4 drawing IDs so I reported 4 designs as done. But I never opened the workspace to confirm all 4 were actually on the canvas. Only 1 was visible.
fix: After any Vizcom agent session, ALWAYS open the workbench in the browser and take a screenshot to visually confirm every output before marking the step complete or reporting results to the user.

summary: Called illustration-style sketches 'designs' without running the render step to produce photorealistic product images
root_cause: TEXT_TO_IMAGE produces concept sketches, not product renders. The RENDER step is required to make outputs look like real products. I skipped this and marked the step complete.
fix: design_explore is NOT complete until: (1) sketches are generated, (2) RENDER is run on each sketch, (3) rendered outputs are visually confirmed in the browser. Only then log step as complete.

summary: Vizcom agent generated unicorn-themed Game Boy instead of brushed metal collector device on hero shot
root_cause: Prompt referenced prior design context loosely — agent invented its own interpretation instead of staying on brief
fix: For final renders, describe the design explicitly in the prompt (materials, colors, exact details) rather than referencing prior session designs. Do not assume agent has context from previous sessions.
